author: nero_eth
content:
Looking at the BAL json rpc methods, we have the following specs:

**User-facing**
  • `eth_getBlockAccessList(number|tag|hash)` -> json access list (array of account changes), `null` if block unknown or `pending` - errors: `-32001` pre-Amsterdam, `4444` pruned
  • `debug_getRawBlockAccessList(number|tag|hash)` -> raw rlp bytes, `0xc0` when empty - errors: `-32001`, `4444`

  **Engine API**
  • `engine_newPayloadV5` - `ExecutionPayloadV4.blockAccessList` (rlp data) required, `-32602` if missing
  • `engine_getPayloadV6` - returns `ExecutionPayloadV4` incl. `blockAccessList`
  • `engine_getPayloadBodiesByHashV2` / `...ByRangeV2`- each body carries `blockAccessList: DATA | null` (`null` = pre-Amsterdam or pruned)                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                        
  **Block**                                                        
  • `eth_getBlockByNumber/Hash` include `blockAccessListHash` for Amsterdam blocks

Here's a summary where client still differ in their BAL specific json rpc implementation based on glamsterdam-devnet-7:
https://hackmd.io/@Nerolation/Hk1WTpuVze

Right now, every client deviates from the specs a here and there.
Also created a PR that clarified certain points where clients differed:
https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/851

cc <@&1460290804898861117>
